name: Vercel AI Gateway
title: Vercel AI Gateway integration: replay on your key
description: Use your Vercel AI Gateway key to discover cheaper candidates live, replay real steps at temperature 0, judge quality, and record provider-reported costs.
lede: Vercel AI Gateway is a shipped replay route in rightmodeler. Candidate calls and the cross-family judge run through the Gateway on your key, against models discovered from its live catalog.
tldr: Set AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY and rightmodeler can use the Vercel AI Gateway for the full audit: preflight checks your credit balance, /models supplies current prices and capabilities, /chat/completions runs replays and the cross-family judge at temperature 0, and provider-reported usage or generation records price each call. If a generation record cannot be fetched, the report marks the catalog-and-usage fallback as an estimate.

steps.label: 01 · Key
steps.title: Check the key and balance before spending
steps.body: Export AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY, or keep it in a .env that the skill can find. Preflight calls the Gateway's /credits endpoint and prints the balance and total used before any replay or judge call spends your credits. Set RIGHTMODELER_PROVIDER only when several replay provider keys are configured.

steps.label: 02 · Catalog
steps.title: Discover candidates from the live catalog
steps.body: GET /models supplies per-token input and output pricing, context windows, and capability tags such as tool use and structured output. rightmodeler normalizes those fields and builds the shortlist at run time, so there is no fixed model list to go stale.

steps.label: 03 · Replay + judge
steps.title: Replay at temperature 0, judge across families
steps.body: Each candidate replays the step's recorded inputs through POST /chat/completions at temperature 0. A judge from a model family different from both the candidate and the model you run today scores the result against the output you shipped; if a neutral family cannot be resolved, the audit requires an explicit judge instead of guessing.

steps.label: 04 · Price
steps.title: Use the Gateway's charge for each call
steps.body: rightmodeler records provider-reported usage cost when the response includes it. Otherwise it fetches the Gateway's generation record by response id and reads total_cost. If that record cannot be fetched, token usage and live catalog prices produce a fallback that the report labels as an estimate.

setup.intro: Use a Vercel AI Gateway key with credits. Preflight verifies the account endpoint and live model catalog before the audit begins; choose the provider explicitly only when more than one provider is configured.

commands.comment: # replay and judge calls bill to your Vercel AI Gateway credits
# only needed when several provider keys are set
# then point the skill at any exported trace

useCases.title: Audit on the route you already fund
useCases.body: If your team already has Vercel AI Gateway credits, the same account can fund candidate replays and judge calls. Preflight shows the balance before the audit starts spending.

useCases.title: Test against a catalog that stays current
useCases.body: The shortlist comes from the Gateway's live /models response, including current pricing, context windows, and capability tags. New and retired models change the next run without a rightmodeler release.

useCases.title: Keep capability checks in the shortlist
useCases.body: Tool-use and structured-output tags become capability filters, and context windows are checked before a candidate reaches replay. A lower price alone is not enough to make the bench.

useCases.title: Confirm complex swaps through the same route
useCases.body: For end-to-end replay, the throwaway worktree points OPENAI_BASE_URL at the Vercel AI Gateway inside the sandbox, so the candidate pipeline runs through the selected replay provider without changing your real worktree.

limits: Replay and judge calls spend your Vercel AI Gateway credits. Preflight reports the available balance first, but the evidence is produced with real billable calls.

A generation record is the preferred cost authority when usage cost is absent from the response. If the record cannot be fetched, rightmodeler falls back to token usage and live catalog pricing, and the report labels that amount as an estimate.
A cheaper candidate is not automatically a recommendation. High-risk steps are held, missing capabilities are filtered out, and weak or inconsistent evidence ends in abstention.

rightmodeler is an offline report on traces you provide, never a runtime gateway or a hop in your production request path. Replay inputs still leave your machine for the Gateway when the audit calls a model.
End-to-end replay points OPENAI_BASE_URL at the Vercel AI Gateway only inside a throwaway sandbox. It does not rewrite your application's production routing.

faq.q: Do I need a Vercel AI Gateway account?
faq.a: Yes. You provide AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY and fund the replay and judge calls with your own Gateway credits. Preflight checks /credits and shows the balance and total used before the audit starts spending.

faq.q: Why route the audit through the Vercel AI Gateway?
faq.a: It gives rightmodeler one OpenAI-compatible route plus a live model catalog with pricing, context windows, and capability tags. That is enough to shortlist cheaper capable models, replay them, and choose a judge from a neutral family without a fixed model list.

rightmodeler uses provider-reported usage cost when it is present. Otherwise it fetches the Gateway generation record for the response and reads total_cost. If that lookup fails, it calculates a fallback from token usage and current catalog prices and labels the result as an estimate.

faq.q: Does my trace data leave my machine?
faq.a: The audit and report run locally, but the recorded step inputs used for replays and judging are sent to the Vercel AI Gateway with your key. There is no rightmodeler server and the audit does not add anything to your production request path.

No, and that is the point. Some steps have no cheaper capable candidate, some candidates miss the quality floor, and weak or inconsistent evidence causes the audit to abstain. A tool that always finds a swap is not measuring anything.

faq.q: If I approve a swap, must I keep using the Gateway?
faq.a: No. The report recommends a model, not a permanent route. The Gateway is the test bench for this audit; you can serve an approved model through the provider or gateway that fits your application.
